rationale: Ding (2007) is used in the citing paper's core framing, not just listed as generic background: the authors explicitly cite it for the claim that incentive alignment induces truth-telling. They also include it among key prior studies showing improved external validity in incentive-aligned conjoint settings, which motivates their own research question about what Prob changes beyond truth-telling. In Study 3, they further use it as a benchmark for practical incentive-design parameters, reinforcing a methodological grounding role.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Ding (2007) as a foundational prior result on incentive-aligned conjoint mechanisms (truth-telling and validity gains), then builds its contribution by examining additional behavioral consequences of realization probability. Thus, Ding (2007) provides the baseline literature position that this paper develops further.
